description:
src/lib.rs:231-248 is_not_found and is_transient are pure functions that classify azure_core::Error. They are critical to caller retry behavior, but no unit tests cover them. Integration tests are gated on SECRETX_AZURE_TEST=1 and require a live vault.

Maintenance consequence: a workspace upgrade of azure_core that renames or restructures ErrorKind variants (e.g. adds Connection — see issue crate-secretsx-bdu0.2) will compile fine and ship a regression. CI cannot catch it.

Fix: add unit tests that construct azure_core::Error values via the public constructors (Error::with_message, Error::new with HttpResponse kind via the response error-builder helpers) and assert is_not_found/is_transient produce the expected classification. azure_core's test infrastructure or a hand-rolled minimal RawResponse should be sufficient. If azure_core's API does not let you construct an HttpResponse error from outside, file a follow-up to upstream; in the interim, factor the classifier to take ErrorKind directly so the test can construct the kind without the wrapper.
title: is_not_found / is_transient have no unit tests
